title: Latency spike on StreamOps pipeline
summary: Processing latency spiked to 2,340ms (threshold: 200ms) across kafka-consumer and flink-operator components. Correlated with heap usage at 92% on the TaskManager, suggesting GC pressure as the root cause.
root_cause: GC pressure on Flink TaskManager causing processing delays. Heap at 92% with 450ms GC pause times correlating with latency spikes.
timeline: T+0s: Latency spike scenario injected by simulator
T+5s: MetricGenerator begins emitting latency_ms values in 2000-5000ms range
T+10s: AnomalyDetector EMA crosses 200ms threshold, fires alert
T+15s: Agent monitoring cycle detects anomaly via Flink job status and consumer lag
T+18s: Diagnostic Agent investigates with query_flink_jobs, query_metrics, get_consumer_lag
T+22s: Report Agent produces incident report with HIGH severity

recommended_actions.rationale: Current heap is insufficient for state size, causing frequent GC pauses that block processing

recommended_actions.action: Enable incremental checkpointing to reduce state snapshot size
recommended_actions.rationale: Full checkpoints contribute to memory pressure by pinning state during snapshot

monitoring_notes: After heap increase, monitor heap_usage_percent and gc_pause_ms for 30 minutes. Latency should drop below 200ms within 2-3 checkpoint cycles.
